title: Bothell
name: Bothell
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Bothell's life-sciences campuses, Canyon Park business park, and UW Bothell corridor near Seattle.

sections.html: Bothell has built a smaller but distinct commercial identity around life sciences, and a buyer assembling a Bothell identification package should expect biotech-adjacent office and lab space, Canyon Park's business-park inventory, and a smaller multifamily and retail base serving the area around UW Bothell. A qualified intermediary coordinating the closing should confirm which of these a target property belongs to before the 45-day identification window closes, since Bothell's inventory is honestly modest compared with Bellevue or Redmond, and a buyer should size search expectations accordingly rather than expecting Eastside-scale product at Bothell's smaller price points.

sections.heading: Life-Sciences Campuses and Lab Space
sections.html: Bothell's life-sciences presence grew around biotech research and manufacturing space, including facilities tied to Seagen's oncology research before its acquisition by Pfizer, and the lab and office buildings in this cluster carry mechanical and power specifications that a conventional office tenant would not need. A buyer identifying lab space in Bothell should confirm whether existing lab infrastructure transfers value to a new biotech tenant or would need removal for a conventional office use, since that answer changes the pool of tenants who could re-lease the space. UW Bothell's research programs also feed a smaller cluster of early-stage biotech tenants into the surrounding buildings, and a buyer should treat that tenant pool separately from the larger, more established life-sciences employers when assessing credit quality on a rent roll, since an early-stage tenant's funding runway matters more to renewal risk than its lease terms alone would suggest.

sections.heading: Canyon Park's Business-Park Inventory
sections.html: Canyon Park carries a mix of light-industrial, flex, and suburban office buildings serving smaller technology and professional-services tenants who need less space and a lower price point than downtown Bellevue or Redmond can offer. A buyer should confirm whether a Canyon Park building's improvements are configured for office, flex, or light-industrial use, since Canyon Park's inventory mixes all three more than a single-use business park would. Parking ratios and loading configuration should be checked building by building here, since a business park assembled over several development phases rarely applies a single consistent specification across every building.

sections.heading: UW Bothell and the Surrounding Retail and Multifamily Base
sections.html: The area around UW Bothell supports a smaller multifamily and neighborhood retail base drawing on students, faculty, and staff, and this product trades at a lower basis than the biotech-adjacent office space closer to the life-sciences cluster. A buyer replacing into this pocket should confirm current rent-cap treatment on the target building's vintage, the same requirement that applies anywhere else in the metro. This is also the pocket of Bothell where a buyer is most likely to find a small multi-tenant retail strip suited to a modest exchange rather than a large institutional-scale replacement property.

sections.heading: Building the Bothell Identification List
sections.html:
A workable Bothell submittal typically draws from:

  • life-sciences office and lab space
  • Canyon Park flex, light-industrial, or suburban office buildings
  • multifamily near UW Bothell
  • neighborhood retail serving the surrounding residential population

sections.heading: Confirming Scale Before Finalizing an Identification
sections.html:
Because Bothell's inventory is smaller than the larger Eastside submarkets, a buyer should confirm early whether a target property's diligence timeline and lender requirements match what is realistic for Bothell's scale, rather than assuming a Bellevue-sized transaction timeline applies.

Buyers frequently pair a Bothell life-sciences identification with a Redmond or Kirkland alternative on the same three-property list, so a longer lab-infrastructure review on one candidate does not leave the exchange without an option inside the 180-day exchange period. A qualified intermediary should also confirm that a smaller Bothell purchase price does not itself create boot if it sits below the value of the relinquished property, since basis matching still matters on a smaller-scale replacement the same way it would on a larger one.

faqs.q: What kind of life-sciences tenants has Bothell attracted?
faqs.a: Biotech research and office tenants, including facilities tied to Seagen's oncology research before its acquisition by Pfizer, which anchor a lab-and-office cluster with specialized mechanical infrastructure, along with a smaller ring of earlier-stage research tenants connected to UW Bothell.

faqs.q: What should a buyer confirm before identifying lab space in Bothell?
faqs.a: Whether the existing lab infrastructure transfers value to a new biotech tenant or would need removal for conventional office use, since that changes the pool of tenants able to re-lease the building at the end of the current term.

faqs.q: What kind of buildings make up Canyon Park's inventory?
faqs.a: A mix of light-industrial, flex, and suburban office buildings, so a buyer should confirm how a specific building's improvements are configured before comparing it to a single-use business park, since parking and loading specifications vary from one development phase to the next.

faqs.q: Is Bothell multifamily subject to the same rent-cap law as the rest of the metro?
faqs.a: Yes, and a buyer should confirm how it applies to a target building's specific vintage near UW Bothell before finalizing an identification, since the requirement does not change based on the property's smaller scale or its distance from the life-sciences cluster.

faqs.q: Why pair a Bothell identification with Redmond or Kirkland?
faqs.a: Lab-infrastructure review on a life-sciences building can take longer than standard diligence, so pairing it with a nearby alternative keeps the exchange on track inside the 180-day period, and either alternative gives a buyer a faster-moving, more conventional office or retail comparable to fall back on if the Bothell candidate's review runs long.
